title: Workflow frontmatter
_comment: Schema for workflow primitives. A workflow is a named, repeatable process: ordered steps, owners, approvals, handoffs, failure modes, edge cases. Downstream consumers (runbook generators, agent task planners, future workflow-rebuild aggregator) depend on these fields. Malformed YAML silently drops the workflow from automation. Includes the cross-type frontmatter contract (provenance, confidence, freshness_days, last_verified, source_count, memory_class, entity_ids) shared across all 8 typed memory primitives. memory_class default for workflow is procedural. entity_ids cross-source linking (slack, github_pr, github_issue, jira, notion, gmail, linear).

owner.description: Person or role accountable for the workflow as a whole.

steps.description: Ordered list of steps in the workflow.

approvals.description: Approval gates required to advance the workflow.

handoffs.description: Transitions between owners, with the event that triggers each transition.

failure_modes.description: Known ways the workflow fails, paired with the documented mitigation.

edge_cases.description: Atypical inputs and the branch the workflow takes for each.

last_verified.description: ISO 8601 date when the workflow was last confirmed.

memory_class.description: Episodic captures one-time events; procedural captures repeatable, time-stable knowledge. Default for workflow is procedural.

entity_ids.description: Cross-source identifiers. Keys: slack, github_pr, github_issue, jira, notion, gmail, linear. Values: source-system IDs.
